title: The Software Crash In Numbers: These Stocks Are Getting Hit Hardest
author: Piero Cingari
description: The software sector is experiencing one of its worst drawdowns in years, with the iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF (IGV) down 16% over seven consecutive sessions. Year-to-date, 100 of 110 constituents are in negative territory, with over 20 stocks down more than 30%. The selloff is attributed to AI disruption pressuring software business models, though some industry leaders like Nvidia's CEO argue AI depends on software tools rather than replacing them.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Down 16% over seven consecutive sessions, worst streak since January 2024, with 97 of 110 constituents trading below 200-day moving average and RSI at lowest levels since September 2001
Largest YTD loser in the software sector, down 49.7% year-to-date
Down 45.3% year-to-date with RSI at 17.1, indicating deeply oversold conditions

Major SaaS platform facing pricing pressure and weaker customer retention, RSI at 18.9
Deeply oversold with RSI at 19.7, indicating severe technical weakness
Extremely oversold with RSI at 11.8, the lowest among mentioned stocks

title: Intapp President Dumps 75% of Direct Holdings in $223,000 December Sale
author: Sara Appino
description: David Benjamin Harrison, President of Industries at Intapp, sold 4,960 shares (75% of his direct holdings) on December 3, 2025, for approximately $223,100. The sale occurred as Intapp stock has declined 32.76% over the past year amid concerns about growth deceleration during its transition from on-premise to cloud-based SaaS solutions. Despite beating Q1 fiscal 2026 earnings expectations, the company remains unprofitable and faces moderating growth rates.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Executive insider sold 75% of direct holdings amid significant stock decline (-32.76% YTD, -50% from 52-week high). Company faces growth deceleration concerns, legacy revenue decline, and remains unprofitable despite cloud transition efforts. The substantial reduction in insider holdings suggests lack of confidence in near-term prospects.

title: Is Workiva’s Slide Creating an Opening for Long-Term Investors as a Major Fund Initiates a New Stake?
author: Jonathan Ponciano
description: Praesidium Investment Management Company initiated a new $7.6 million stake in Workiva during Q3, despite the stock's 7.5% decline over the past year. Workiva demonstrated strong financial performance with 21% revenue growth and improving profitability.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Despite stock underperformance, the company showed strong revenue growth (21%), improved profitability (from $17M loss to $3M profit), and expanding non-GAAP operating margins, suggesting potential future growth

title: Intapp DealCloud named Deal Origination Solution of the Year: Credit at the Private Equity Wire European Awards 2025
author: N/A
description: Intapp DealCloud, an AI-powered platform for private credit firms, was named the Deal Origination Solution of the Year at the Private Equity Wire European Awards 2025, recognized for its ability to help firms source and close deals more efficiently.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article highlights Intapp's DealCloud platform being recognized as the Deal Origination Solution of the Year, indicating the company's success in developing innovative solutions for the private equity market.

title: Intapp to Participate in Upcoming Investor Conference
author: N/A
description: Intapp, a provider of AI-powered solutions for professional firms, announced that its senior management will present at the upcoming UBS Global Technology and AI Conference in Scottsdale, Arizona on December 3, 2024.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article announces that Intapp's senior management will present at an upcoming investor conference, which suggests the company is actively engaging with investors and showcasing its capabilities.
